title: Antilla bahorucensis
intro: Antilla bahorucensis, also known as Bahoruc, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Bahoruc include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into various remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and creams, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
